Package name: gbrain.

Available npm scripts: `dev`, `build`, `build:all`, `build:admin`, `build:schema`, `build:llms`, `build:pglite-snapshot`, `test`, `test:full`, `verify`, `check:admin-scope-drift`, `check:cli-exec`, `check:all`, `check:wasm`, `check:newlines`, `test:e2e`, `test:slow`, `test:profile`, `test:serial`, `ci:local`, `ci:local:diff`, `ci:select-e2e`, `typecheck`, `check:jsonb`, `check:privacy`, `check:progress`, `check:exports-count`, `check:admin-build`, `check:test-isolation`, `postinstall`, `prepublish:clawhub`, `publish:clawhub`.

README excerpt:
# GBrain

Your AI agent is smart but forgetful. GBrain gives it a brain.

Built by the President and CEO of Y Combinator to run his actual AI agents. The production brain powering his OpenClaw and Hermes deployments: **17,888 pages, 4,383 people, 723 companies**, 21 cron jobs running autonomously, built in 12 days. The agent ingests meetings, emails, tweets, voice calls, and original ideas while you sleep. It enriches every person and company it encounters. It fixes its own citations and consolidates memory overnight. You wake up and the brain is smarter than when you went to bed.

The brain wires itself. Every page write extracts entity references and creates typed links (`attended`, `works_at`, `invested_in`, `founded`, `advises`) with zero LLM calls. Hybrid search. Self-wiring knowledge graph. Structured timeline. Backlink-boosted ranking. Ask "who works at Acme AI?" or "what did Bob invest in this quarter?" and get answers vector search alone can't reach. Benchmarked side-by-side a

context.fact: Generated repo overview summarizes package metadata and the README as a navigation aid for agent startup.
context.why: Agents need fast repo orientation before deeper recall or code graph queries, but generated overview memory should stay separate from human rationale.
context.trigger: Recall when an agent needs first-pass repo purpose, scripts, stack, or README context.
context.action: Use this as orientation only, then inspect source-backed memory and code graph facts for implementation decisions.
context.verification: Generated from package.json and README.md when present.
context.risk_if_forgotten: Agents may waste context rediscovering basic repo purpose or treat generated overview text as deeper semantic memory.
context.stale_when: package.json or README.md changes enough that the generated overview no longer matches the repo.
